burial.plot_notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, Manistee Manistee Co MI — same cemetery where his uncle Dr. Lathrop Stiles Ellis (HD4244) is buried.

naming_inspiration.description: First name Asahel — common Strong family name (the Northampton MA Strong-Hooker-descent line has multiple Asahels, including Capt. Asahel Strong Jr. I00055 who married Sarah Hooker H00101). May honor a Strong family connection through paternal grandmother Ruth Strong Lyman.

narrative_blocks.header: First Hooker-Talcott cross-line marriage on record
narrative_blocks.body: Asahel Hubert Lyman married Julia Elizabeth Barnard Lyman — a Talcott descendant through her mother Julia Hobart Barnard and grandmother Betsey Perrin Hobart. Their marriage represents the first documented union of the two great descendant lines that form the project's twin axes — Thomas Hooker's descendants meeting John Talcott's descendants in a single household. Asahel descended from Hooker through the Stiles-Ellis-Lyman line; Julia descended from Talcott through the Perrin-Hobart-Barnard line.

narrative_blocks.header: Orphaned at eleven, died Manistee Michigan at 43
narrative_blocks.body: Asahel Hubert Lyman was orphaned at age eleven — his mother Luthena Ellis Lyman died at Northampton when he was eight, and his father was killed at Fredericksburg Virginia during Grant's Overland Campaign three years later. He migrated west to Manistee Township Michigan and died there in 1896 at age forty-three, buried at Oak Grove Cemetery alongside where his maternal uncle Dr. Lathrop Stiles Ellis would be interred four years later.

burial.plot_notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, Manistee Manistee Co MI — same cemetery where his uncle Dr. Lathrop Stiles Ellis (HD4244) is buried.

narrative_blocks.header: Orphaned at eleven, died Manistee Michigan at 43
narrative_blocks.body: Asahel Hubert Lyman was orphaned at age eleven — his mother Luthena Ellis Lyman died at Northampton when he was eight, and his father was killed at Fredericksburg Virginia during Grant's Overland Campaign three years later. He migrated west to Manistee Township Michigan and died there in 1896 at age forty-three, buried at Oak Grove Cemetery alongside where his maternal uncle Dr. Lathrop Stiles Ellis would be interred four years later.

burial.plot_notes: Pleasant Street Cemetery, Greenville Hillsborough Co NH.

narrative_blocks.header: Bridged Hooker and Talcott lines through marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Julia Elizabeth Barnard's marriage to Asahel Hubert Lyman bridged the two great descendant lines that anchor the entire project — her own Talcott descent through the Perrin-Hobart-Barnard line meeting his Hooker descent through the Stiles-Ellis-Lyman line. She was the youngest of three Barnard sisters, daughter of the Vermont pastor Rev. Pliny Fisk Barnard.

narrative_blocks.header: Widowed at forty-three, returned east alone
narrative_blocks.body: Asahel Hubert Lyman died at Manistee Township Michigan in 1896 at age forty-three, leaving Julia a childless widow at the same age. She returned east to New England and lived for thirty-three more years, dying at Greenville New Hampshire in 1929. She is buried at Pleasant Street Cemetery Greenville — far from her husband's Michigan grave at Oak Grove Manistee.

death.cause: Killed in action / died of wounds during Grant's Overland Campaign

burial.plot_notes: Norwich Hill Cemetery Huntington MA — body returned home from Virginia after Civil War death.

parents.father_research_notes: Deacon John Burt Lyman Sr. (1792-1876) — per FA #121993556. Not built per Jalapeño Rule.
parents.mother_research_notes: Ruth Strong Lyman (1783-1856) — per FA #121993530. POTENTIAL HOOKER DESCENDANT through the extensive Strong family Hooker connections at Northampton MA (43 Strong-surname Hooker descendants in tree). Sam flagged 'cousins through ruth maybe?' — if Ruth Strong descends from the Asahel Strong / Sarah Hooker Strong line, this marriage could be a within-tree cousin marriage. Lineage of this Ruth Strong (b.1783) not yet verified. Not built per Jalapeño Rule pending research.

military_service.unit: Massachusetts regiment (specific unit undocumented; Hampshire County MA recruitment)
military_service.notes: Civil War veteran (per FA 'Veteran' designation). Died of wounds May 22, 1864 at Fredericksburg Virginia field hospital depot — one day after the Battle of Spotsylvania Court House officially ended (May 8-21). Wounded at Spotsylvania during Grant's Overland Campaign and transported to the Fredericksburg hospital depot, where he died. The Overland Campaign timeline: Battle of the Wilderness May 5-7, Spotsylvania Court House May 8-21, North Anna May 23-26 — Fredericksburg was the major Union field hospital depot receiving wounded from all these battles.

narrative_blocks.header: Civil War death at Fredericksburg, May 22 1864
narrative_blocks.body: John Burt Lyman Jr was wounded at Spotsylvania Court House during Grant's Overland Campaign and died of his wounds at the Fredericksburg Virginia field hospital depot on May 22, 1864 — one day after the battle officially ended. He was thirty-eight years old. His body was returned to Massachusetts and buried at Norwich Hill Cemetery Huntington.

narrative_blocks.header: Three young orphans left by his battlefield death
narrative_blocks.body: His wife Luthena had died at Northampton three years earlier in August 1861. His Civil War death therefore left their three children fully orphaned — the eldest son Asahel Hubert only eleven, the youngest daughter Catherine 'Katie' just four. Their middle child Elbert had already died in infancy in 1851.

burial.plot_notes: Norwich Hill Cemetery, Huntington Hampshire Co MA — with husband John Burt Lyman Jr (KIA Civil War 1864) and daughter Catherine Elizabeth 'Katie' Lyman.

narrative_blocks.header: Died young Northampton, three years before husband
narrative_blocks.body: Luthena Elizabeth Ellis Lyman died at Northampton Massachusetts on August 26, 1861 at age thirty-three, leaving her husband John Burt Lyman Jr a widower with three young children — the youngest, Catherine 'Katie,' only two years old. Three years later her husband was killed at Fredericksburg Virginia during the Civil War, orphaning all three children.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter of Samuel Ellis, twice-widowed farmer
narrative_blocks.body: Luthena's mother Betsey Kirkland Ellis died at twenty-eight in 1832, when Luthena was four — her father Samuel Ellis subsequently remarried Sarah Moore of Worthington. Luthena and her older brother Lathrop, both born in 1828, were the only children of Samuel's first marriage to survive infancy; her two half-siblings from the second marriage both died young.

burial.plot_notes: Dawes Cemetery Cummington Hampshire Co MA — with second wife Sarah Moore Ellis.

career.notes: Farmer at Goshen Mass. per Edward Ellis Salisbury's 1885 family biographical compilation.

narrative_blocks.header: Twice-widowed farmer, four children across marriages
narrative_blocks.body: Samuel Ellis was a farmer at Goshen and Cummington Massachusetts. He married first Betsey Kirkland in 1825, who died at age twenty-eight in 1832; they had twin children Lathrop Stiles and Luthena Elizabeth, both born February 14, 1828. He later married Sarah Moore of Worthington MA, with whom he had two more children — Jane Betsey (1835-1851, age sixteen) and John Holland (1839-1860, age twenty-one) — both of whom died young.

burial.plot_notes: Ellis Cemetery, Huntington Hampshire County MA.

burialCemetery.name: Oak Grove Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, Manistee Manistee County MI. Burial site of Dr. Lathrop Stiles Ellis (HD4244, physician) and his wife Sarah Weston Harmon Ellis.
